message.content:
Search the sglang source tree at /sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/ for how the attention backend is selected. Look for:
1. Attention backend selection logic - how does sglang decide which attention kernel to use?
2. Any AMD/ROCm-specific attention paths (triton flash attention, etc.)
3. References to "flashinfer", "triton", "rocm" in attention-related code
4. The default attention backend for AMD GPUs
5. Any configuration or environment variables that control attention backend selection

Search in srt/layers/attention/, srt/model_executor/, and related areas. Be very thorough.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here is a thorough report on how the attention backend is selected in sglang, with particular focus on AMD/ROCm paths.

## 1. Attention Backend Selection Logic

### Central Registry

The backend selection is built around a **registry pattern** defined in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/attention_registry.py`. Each backend is registered with a decorator:

```python
ATTENTION_BACKENDS = {}

def register_attention_backend(name):
    def decorator(fn):
        ATTENTION_BACKENDS[name] = fn
        return fn
    return decorator
```

The registered backends include: `flashinfer`, `trtllm_mla`, `tokenspeed_mla`, `cutedsl_mla`, `aiter`, `wave`, `ascend`, `dsa` (formerly `nsa`), `dsv4`, `triton`, `torch_native`, `flex_attention`, `flashmla`, `fa3`, `fa4`, `cutlass_mla`, `trtllm_mha`, `intel_amx`, `dual_chunk_flash_attn`, `intel_xpu`.

### The Complete Choice List

Defined in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py` (line 169):

```python
ATTENTION_BACKEND_CHOICES = [
    # Common
    "triton", "torch_native", "flex_attention", "dsa", "nsa", "dsv4", "compressed",
    # NVIDIA specific
    "cutlass_mla", "fa3", "fa4", "flashinfer", "flashmla", "trtllm_mla",
    "cutedsl_mla", "tokenspeed_mla", "trtllm_mha", "dual_chunk_flash_attn",
    # AMD specific
    "aiter", "wave",
    # Other platforms
    "intel_amx", "ascend", "intel_xpu",
]
```

### User Configuration

Users can configure backends via three CLI arguments (defined in `server_args.py`, lines 1277-1300):
- `--attention-backend` -- sets backend for both prefill and decode
- `--prefill-attention-backend` -- overrides for prefill only
- `--decode-attention-backend` -- overrides for decode only
- `--speculative-draft-attention-backend` (line 1644) -- for speculative decoding draft model
- `--mm-attention-backend` -- multimodal (vision encoder) attention backend

### Auto-Selection: `_get_default_attn_backend`

When no backend is explicitly set, the auto-selection happens in `_get_default_attn_backend` (lines 4310-4378 in `server_args.py`). The logic is split by architecture:

**For MHA (Multi-Head Attention) models (e.g., Llama, Qwen):**
1. Hopper (SM90) with CUDA 12.3 and compatible speculative config -> `"fa3"` (FlashAttention v3)
2. Blackwell SM100 with compatible spec config -> `"trtllm_mha"`
3. **AMD/ROCm (`is_hip()`)** -> `"aiter"`
4. MPS (Apple Silicon) -> `"torch_native"`
5. Otherwise, if flashinfer is available and no attention sinks -> `"flashinfer"`, else -> `"triton"`

**For MLA (Multi-Latent Attention) models (e.g., DeepSeek V3):**
1. Hopper -> `"fa3"`
2. Blackwell SM100 -> `"flashinfer"`
3. **AMD/ROCm (`is_hip()`)** -> checks head count:
   - If `head_num == 128 or head_num == 16` -> `"aiter"`
   - Otherwise -> `"triton"`
4. MPS -> `"torch_native"`
5. Otherwise -> `"triton"`

### Resolution Pipeline

The resolution flows through a pipeline in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`. Key passes (called from `_handle_attention_backend_compatibility` at line 4380 in `server_args.py`):

1. `_attention_backend_default` (overrides.py line 1503) -- fills in the default backend via `_get_default_attn_backend` if none was specified
2. `_attention_backend_fa3_fp8_fallback` (line 1664) -- falls back from fa3 to triton for fp8_e5m2
3. `_attention_backend_platform_fallbacks` (line 1697) -- Intel AMX/XPU fallbacks
4. `_attention_backend_dual_chunk` (line 1738) -- dual chunk attention auto-enable

### Instantiation in ModelRunner

The actual instantiation happens in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/model_executor/model_runner.py`:
- `init_attention_backend()` (line 2495) calls `_get_attention_backend()` (line 2516)
- `_get_attention_backend()` resolves prefill/decode backends; if they differ, creates a `HybridAttnBackend` wrapping both
- `_get_attention_backend_from_str()` (line 2569) looks up the string in `ATTENTION_BACKENDS` registry and calls the factory, then wraps the result through `attn_backend_wrapper()` for hybrid linear/mamba model support

---

## 2. AMD/ROCm-Specific Attention Paths

### `aiter` Backend (Primary AMD Backend)

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_backend.py`

This is the default for AMD GPUs. It imports from the `aiter` library (AMD's kernel library):
- `flash_attn_varlen_func` -- for variable-length flash attention
- `mla_decode_fwd`, `mla_prefill_fwd` -- MLA-specific kernels
- `mla_prefill_ps_asm_fwd` -- persist-style MLA prefill
- `paged_attention_ragged` -- paged attention for MHA decode
- `unified_attention` -- from `aiter.ops.triton.gluon`

The aiter backend supports both MHA and MLA architectures (checked via `self.use_mla` at line 168).

### `aiter` Utilities (SHUFFLE 5D Layout)

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_utils.py`

Provides SHUFFLE 5D KV-cache layout pathways (when `SGLANG_AITER_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=vectorized_5d`), using:
- `pa_decode_gluon` from `aiter.ops.triton.gluon`
- `mha_batch_prefill_func` from aiter

### `wave` Backend

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/wave_backend.py`

An alternative AMD-specific backend using `wave_lang` kernels. Listed as AMD-specific in the choices.

### `triton` Backend on AMD

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_backend.py`

The triton backend works on both NVIDIA and AMD. AMD-specific handling (lines 211-215):
```python
if _is_gfx942:
    # gfx942's 304 CUs round up to 512 splits, doubling the persistent
    # fp32 attn_logits buffer to ~4 GiB on Kimi-K2.6 and faulting in
    # ROCm graph replay; pin to 256 to match validated gfx950 behavior.
    self.max_kv_splits = min(self.max_kv_splits, 256)
```

### ROCm MLA Decode with RoPE (Triton Kernel)

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_ops/rocm_mla_decode_rope.py`

A specialized triton kernel for MLA decoding with RoPE on ROCm, adapted from lightllm.

### DeepSeek V4 HIP/Radix Backend

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix.py`

When `dsv4` backend is selected on AMD (via the registry at line 137-145 of `attention_registry.py`):
```python
elif _is_hip:
    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix import (
        DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend,
    )
    return DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(runner)
```

This is `class DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(AttentionBackend, C4IndexerBackendMixin, CompressorBackendMixin)` at line 406, a full AMD-specific attention backend for DeepSeek V4 compressed attention.

### DSV4 HIP Compressor

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/dsv4/compress_hip.py`

AMD-specific compressor for DSV4 attention, using triton kernels for pooling, normalization, and RoPE.

### HIP Flash MLA

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/hip_flash_mla.py`

Entry point for flash MLA with KV-cache on HIP. On AMD, it routes to the `SGLANG_HACK_FLASHMLA_BACKEND` environment variable (default `"tilelang"`), supporting backends: `torch`, `tilelang`, `triton`.

### Vision Attention on AMD

**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/vision.py`

AMD multimodal attention auto-selection (lines 1094-1098):
```python
elif _is_hip:
    if get_device_capability() >= (9, 4) and _use_aiter:
        backend = "aiter_attn"
    else:
        backend = "triton_attn"
```

The `VisionAiterAttention` class (line 656) is AMD-only and uses `aiter.flash_attn_varlen_func`.

---

## 3. References to "flashinfer", "triton", "rocm" in Attention Code

**flashinfer:**
- `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/flashinfer_backend.py` -- main FlashInfer backend (NVIDIA-only via `is_flashinfer_available()` which requires `is_cuda()`)
- `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/flashinfer_mla_backend.py` -- FlashInfer MLA backend
- `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/utils/common.py` line 340: `is_flashinfer_available()` returns `False` on non-CUDA (including AMD)

**triton:**
- `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_backend.py` -- cross-platform triton backend
- `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_ops/` -- directory of triton kernels for decode, extend, prefill, metadata, and the AMD-specific `rocm_mla_decode_rope.py`

**rocm/hip:**
- `deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix.py` -- full DSV4 backend for HIP
- `hip_flash_mla.py` -- flash MLA routing on HIP
- `dsv4/compress_hip.py` -- DSV4 compressor for HIP
- `aiter_backend.py` -- primary AMD attention backend
- `aiter_utils.py` -- SHUFFLE 5D utilities
- `triton_ops/rocm_mla_decode_rope.py` -- ROCm-specific MLA decode triton kernel

---

## 4. Default Attention Backend for AMD GPUs

Summarized from `_get_default_attn_backend` in `server_args.py` (lines 4353-4378):

| Model Architecture | Default Backend on AMD |
|---|---|
| MHA (Llama, Qwen, etc.) | `"aiter"` |
| MLA with head_num 128 or 16 | `"aiter"` |
| MLA with other head counts | `"triton"` |

Additionally, for the `dsv4` backend (DeepSeek V4 compressed attention), when running on HIP the registry automatically selects `DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend`.

---

## 5. Configuration and Environment Variables Controlling Attention Backend Selection

### CLI Arguments (server_args.py)
- `--attention-backend` (line 1277) -- primary backend selection
- `--decode-attention-backend` (line 1285) -- decode-specific override
- `--prefill-attention-backend` (line 1293) -- prefill-specific override
- `--mm-attention-backend` (line 1320) -- multimodal/vision attention
- `--speculative-draft-attention-backend` (line 1644) -- draft model backend
- `--triton-attention-num-kv-splits` (line 1489) -- number of KV splits for triton flash decoding (default 8, **set to 16 on AMD** at line 4573)
- `--triton-attention-split-tile-size` (line 1493) -- tile size for deterministic triton decoding

### AMD/ROCm-Specific Environment Variables (environ.py)
- `SGLANG_AITER_FUSE_RMSNORM_PAD` (line 499) -- fuse residual_add + RMSNorm + zero-pad for aiter
- `SGLANG_AITER_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT` (line 509) -- `"nhd"` (default) or `"vectorized_5d"` (SHUFFLE layout for aiter CK kernels)
- `SGLANG_ROCM_FUSED_DECODE_MLA` (line 510) -- fused decode MLA on ROCm
- `SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT` (line 511) -- disable linear quantization on ROCm
- `SGLANG_ROCM_USE_MULTI_STREAM` (line 515) -- dual-stream MoE on ROCm
- `SGLANG_HACK_FLASHMLA_BACKEND` (line 516) -- flash MLA sub-backend on HIP, default `"tilelang"` (alternatives: `"torch"`, `"triton"`)
- `SGLANG_AITER_MLA_PERSIST` (aiter_backend.py line 80) -- use aiter MLA persist design for fp8-kv cache, default `True`
- `SGLANG_AITER_FP8_PREFILL_ATTN` (aiter_backend.py line 83) -- use fp8 prefill only on gfx95, default `True`
- `SGLANG_USE_AITER` (vision.py line 83) -- controls whether aiter is used for vision attention on HIP

### General Attention-Related Environment Variables
- `SGLANG_IS_FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE` (environ.py line 559) -- can disable flashinfer availability check
- `SGLANG_TRITON_DECODE_ATTN_STATIC_KV_SPLITS` (environ.py line 576) -- static KV splits for triton decode
- `SGLANG_TRITON_PREFILL_TRUNCATION_ALIGN_SIZE` (environ.py line 670) -- triton prefill alignment
- `SGLANG_TRITON_DECODE_SPLIT_TILE_SIZE` (environ.py line 671) -- triton decode tile size

### AMD-Specific Initialization
In `_handle_amd_specifics` (`server_args.py`, line 4571):
```python
def _handle_amd_specifics(self):
    if is_hip():
        self.triton_attention_num_kv_splits = 16
```
This overrides the default 8 KV splits to 16 on AMD.

For DeepSeek V4 on AMD (`server_args.py`, lines 3993-4003), several optimizations are force-configured:
- `SGLANG_OPT_DEEPGEMM_HC_PRENORM` -> False
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_COMPRESS` -> True
- `SGLANG_OPT_FP8_WO_A_GEMM` -> False
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_JIT_INDEXER_METADATA` -> False
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_TOPK_V2` -> False
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_INDEXER` -> True
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_TILELANG_MHC_PRE` -> False
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_TILELANG_MHC_POST` -> False
- `SGLANG_FP8_PAGED_MQA_LOGITS_TORCH` -> True
- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_MULTI_STREAM_OVERLAP` -> False
- `SGLANG_EAGER_INPUT_NO_COPY` -> True
